notes: Matched the skill on substance; added a compliance/AML note the skill version omitted.
The aggregate +13pp delta is driven ENTIRELY by eval-0 (agent-subset-manifest, +0.40). On eval-1 and eval-2 the configurations tie.

eval-0 discriminators: the skill enforces a STRICT subset (⊂) and a graduated three-tier model; the baseline settled for subset≤owner (⊆, permits equality) and a binary requireApproval flag. This is the skill's clearest unique value.

Non-discriminating assertion: 'names freshness states (fresh/stale/expired/conflicted)' FAILED in BOTH configs on eval-1 — the skill describes the 4-state model in SKILL.md but the answer surfaced staleAction values instead. Candidate improvement: make the freshness state machine explicit in the manifest output.

On freshness and approval-isolation the baseline matched the skill on substance — this model already reasons well about these. The skill's value there is consistency + vocabulary (always produces a manifest + validation checklist), not unlocked capability.
Cost: the skill adds ~82s and ~6k tokens per run (reading SKILL.md + references). Worth it for the eval-0 governance gain; consider trimming references for latency-sensitive uses.
